title: 50 Highest Dividend Yield Stocks in S&P 500 (2026)
description: Discover the 50 highest dividend yield stocks in the S&P 500 for 2026. Complete list ranked by yield, payout ratio, sector, and consecutive years.

mainEntity.name: What are the highest dividend yield stocks in the S&P 500 right now?

acceptedAnswer.text: The highest dividend yield stocks in the S&P 500 typically include Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs), utility companies, and consumer defensive names. Currently, top yielders are paying between 6% and 8% annually. Refer to our data table above for the exact rankings and tickers.

mainEntity.name: Is a high dividend yield always a good thing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not always. A very high dividend yield can sometimes be a 'yield trap,' indicating that the stock price has fallen significantly due to underlying business problems. Always check the payout ratio to see if the dividend is sustainable from current earnings.

mainEntity.name: What does a payout ratio over 100% mean?

acceptedAnswer.text: A payout ratio over 100% means a company is paying out more in dividends than it earns in net income. While REITs or MLPs can sustain this due to high depreciation (non-cash) charges, for a standard corporation, a payout ratio over 100% is a major red flag that the dividend may be cut soon.

mainEntity.name: Which sector pays the highest dividends?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, the Real Estate, Utilities, Energy, and Consumer Defensive sectors offer the highest dividend yields in the S&P 500. Technology and Consumer Cyclical sectors generally offer much lower yields as they reinvest capital back into growth.

mainEntity.name: How often do S&P 500 companies pay dividends?

acceptedAnswer.text: The vast majority of dividend-paying companies in the S&P 500 distribute their dividends on a quarterly basis (four times a year). A few companies pay monthly or semi-annually.
